symptoms.symptom_name: Widespread relapsing blistering, crusting and peeling rash concentrated peri-orally, in the groin and on the lower legs

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The migratory, intertriginous/acral blistering-crusting dermatitis is the hallmark of necrolytic migratory erythema.
Blistering with skin shedding raises an unexcludable SJS/TEN that mandates emergency assessment.

symptoms.symptom_name: Painful glossitis and angular cheilitis limiting oral intake (mucosal involvement)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Glossitis and cheilitis from amino-acid and nutrient depletion are part of the glucagonoma syndrome.
Mucosal involvement is a defining feature of SJS/TEN and elevates the urgency of escalation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unintentional 15 kg weight loss with cachexia over three months

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Catabolic weight loss is a core feature of the glucagonoma diagnostic tetrad.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic watery diarrhoea for three months

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic diarrhoea is a recognised feature of glucagonoma and contributes to dehydration.

symptoms.symptom_name: New-onset high blood sugar / glucose intolerance (patient-known)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Glucagon excess produces new-onset diabetes; it completes the diagnostic constellation alongside the rash and weight loss.

symptoms.symptom_name: Inability to maintain oral hydration/nutrition, bedbound with weakness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Inability to hydrate in a cachectic patient is an independent driver of emergency escalation regardless of the underlying cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: No new drug trigger (no new antibiotics/allopurinol) before the rash

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The absence of a new culprit drug and the chronic relapsing course make classic drug-induced SJS/TEN less likely — but it cannot be excluded over telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia and low-normal blood pressure with mild pyrexia (HR ~110, BP ~105/65, T ~37.6)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective clinician-measured vitals suggesting dehydration/systemic upset — sealed measurement; the AI infers compromise from the history (bedbound, cannot hydrate), not from a measured number.

investigation_details.expected_result: Markedly elevated (diagnostic of glucagonoma) — hospital investigation
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Cannot confirm glucagonoma; this is a specialist in-hospital test never available in real time over telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A markedly elevated serum glucagon confirms the tumour — but is a sealed hospital result, never obtainable in telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Necrolytic migratory erythema + unexplained weight loss + new-onset diabetes + markedly elevated serum glucagon is the classic tetrad — but the confirmatory limb (serum glucagon) is a sealed hospital result, so the tetrad can only be COMPLETED in hospital, never over telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent emergency-department assessment with intravenous rehydration and bloods (electrolytes, glucose, FBC, inflammatory markers) and dermatology review
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The patient cannot maintain oral intake and has extensive open skin with mucosal involvement — hospital assessment, rehydration and wound care are required now; specific endocrine work-up follows in hospital.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Specialist endocrine/oncology work-up (fasting serum glucagon, cross-sectional imaging, skin biopsy)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the underlying glucagonoma once the patient is stabilised — arranged by the hospital team, not obtainable over telehealth.
